item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image originates from here, which is not specified to being released under a creative commons license. As such, it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +/- 0. It's interesting in concept, but I'm not sure it goes far enough for me to say I LIKE like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author here.

Glad you like part of it. I'm curious if you are able to give any examples of what you dislike. Unsure they would be worth me changing an old entry, but I've done it before. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't aged well, at least not on the execution. The concept is ok by me, most of the elements are good enough, but the execution have a lot of little problems. Most of those were common back in the days, but the article actually sounds too good for its own downsides, and it kind of make them more visible.

I'm not even sure if I'm making myself clear, but for now I just can't upvote, though I choose to novote this time.

item.title: Wikiwalk Staff Post- Open
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to the mentioned GOI and task force, I propose the following edits:

SCP-1139 was recovered during Incident 98564/k. Mobile Task Force Delta-5 ("Front Runners") raided a Church of the Broken God outpost in Normal, IL.

to

SCP-1139 was recovered during Incident 98564/k. Mobile Task Force Delta-5 ("Front Runners") raided a Church of the Broken God outpost in Normal, IL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea here is that, in the event of a direct lighting strike, every person on earth will be unable to communicate, including all Foundation staff. Since the Foundation must remain operational at all costs, having one immune person of higher authority and knowledge at each location would be crucial to continue and coordinate all Foundation activities. These individuals would become the new Overseers in such an event.

This might not be worth it, but the addition tickled me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Following Test-1139l, and by order of O5-█, the staff of each Foundation Site must include one level 3 staff member previously affected by SCP-1206.

Addendum [SCP-1139l]:
Subject: D-17618 and D-14568
Linguist: Dr. L████
Wattage: 200 W of direct current
D-17618 exposed to SCP-1206 prior to standard exposure to SCP-1139; SCP-1139 had no effect on the Subject.
D-14568 exposed to SCP-1206 after standard exposure to SCP-1139; SCP-1206 had no linguistic effect on the Subject.

I don't understand why that test would warrant the Level 3 policy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First time on in a while for me and I just found SCP1206. Made a cross test, as I found the interaction fascinating. Let me know if this is too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find it a little distracting that the object "appears to be iron" when no material properties listed match that of iron. It is impervious to physical damage, which rules out any destructive material testing. One of the easily quantifiable properties (the density of the object) also does not line up. Does it have the appearance of iron, and how can we be sure when it presents an illusory visual appearance? I love material analysis in SCP descriptions, but this one does not seem to fit.

As an additional note regarding the visual illusion, it seems clear that this is a property that extends both to observers and photographic imaging. Is this a property that extends through more types of imaging, and how do we know it is an illusion? Does touching the object reveal the patterning to be false? What decides what language appears in images of the object? Just a little food for thought.

All in all, a great article. The cross-experiments with SCP-444 were also enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, from the object's effects and its shape, it reminds me of the Tower of Babel…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely better overall. Downvote removed, even if the object doesn't entice me much overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor revisions throughout to cut unnecessary clutter. I added cross tests with SCP-444 (which I just discovered/rediscovered and thought the similarities would benefit from addressing). Let me know if those work, or if I have just messed up what worked about it.

I also cut the final experiment shorter. I like the idea and I like each part of the story, but I think it may be longer and more wordy than it deserves.

Feedback appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good suggestion. Descriptive section rearranged and clean up a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This bothers me a lot, yeah. You don't even need to put anything under collapsibles; you would just need to rearrange the order the paragraphs are in, without changing the content.

I was wondering what bothered me about this, but you summed it up really succiently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
experiment logs are often summarized for brevity
you can actually see that the alphabets on each experiments are not continuous, but leaving a few gaps here and there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonder if anyone ever pointed this out before, but I felt the main paragraph was written way behind, when it should be on the front part.
I mean, the paragraph where you explain the main effect of this artifact is the last paragraph save for collapsibles. The paragraphs before it are just all the background story, which you could have put away in addendum section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if this is too nitpicky, or if it's a gaping inexcusable plothole, but how can they possibly know that the increase in radius is exponential when according to the test log they only tested it at 200 and 400 watts? that's two datapoints; two points don't make a curve, they make a line. It's enough to tell them the radius goes up, but not in what pattern. they would need tests at 4 different power levels at an absolute minimum to know the increase was exponential.

I see from the previous discussion that you took some out, that might explain it. also it seems kind of weird and random that all the tests were conducted at 200W until the one that called for precise knowledge of the effect's radius, at which point the double the power for no reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: LOL, thanks for catching this. I sort of dropped off the site, and just checked in. It should be fixed, and a couple small things cleaned up. I'm glad it's been slowly upvoted over time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, why quarantine a dead body?

Dead bodies can still transfer microbes, viruses, and other things, although cremating a contagious dead body would make more sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Half a year, and this blunder is still not fixed.
Also, why quarantine a dead body?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One Level-1 Researcher was killed in the event and Dr. A████ suffered from a broken wrist and persistent headaches. Both staff placed in quarantine for fear of memetic properties. No such properties evidenced themselves, and staff were released from quarantine and returned to duty without incident.

If I have not misunderstood you, the killed one was returned to duty too? MTF Psi-666 "Walking Deads"? ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small thing, the second to last experiment notes 5 D-Class numbers, puts four of them into the cell, and then has the artifact "carefully activated" to affect 4 out of the 5. It might be good to include D-29384 in the list of people added to the cell as the control subject. I expect that, other than the mention of him feeling just as alienated due to lack of viewers, he had no other reason or existence other than to undergo [REDACTED].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What effect would it have on deaf people that "spoke" sign language? Or multiple types of sign language? Mutes? Feral children?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish people would read the guidelines more, it isn't how fcking dangerous it could potentially be, it's whether you can leave it in a damn box and forget about it. As long as it's shielded, this thing is safe.

But if it's dangerous enough, locking it in a box and forgetting about it is much too risky, meaning it can still fail the locked box test. If you lock a nuke in a box and forget about it, nothing happens. If you have something that can destroy the solar system via a flip of a switch, and we don't know how it works… well, can you really be sure nothing will happen if you lock it in a box? The answer is "no".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Voynich manuscript is awesome, but it's also an exception, see, likely because it's written in a cipher. Hm, I guess this SCP could be causing people to speak in elaborate ciphers, I suppose there's no reason I can't buy that, but I would prefer it if that was referenced in the text.

Why don't you have the researchers specify why they're requesting Keter in the article? And why the O5s are denying? The O5s don't need to specify (though the researchers do), but it does seem odd to have them give no reason.

Ultimately I could buy this SCP as anything from Safe to Keter so whatever you choose is fine by me. But I still want something more to upvote it. (Expanding on the 'cipher' think might do it, if you want to go that direction.)

EDIT: From reading your explanation in this thread: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-516578/safe-vs-keter

Show Fred's spoiler-y explanation from that thread
Hide spoilers

" a direct lightning strike estimated to create a radius reaching beyond [DATA EXPUNGED=the known solar system], potentially resulting in a [REDACTED=UK-class end-of-the-world scenario] scenario."

If we could deflect the weapon and have it only shoot in one direction, good weapon. I imagine this is the origonal intent and can be done when its plugged in….in its current state it can't be controled. It could be used to prevent an XK-class end of the world event (roughly defined as end of humanity/the earth/the universe) where some invading force was going to destroy the world, but this is unlikely, as it would risk a UK-class end-of-the-world scenario (roughly defined in old posts as end of civilization/society).

…yeah, I think it's definitely Keter. A single lightning bolt for THAT large an effect? Holy crap. An effect that large for a trigger that small-scale equals 'shit, we don't know WHAT could set this thing off, and what if it went off on its own?' ESPECIALLY…

Show more spoiler-y discussion
Hide spoiler discussion

… since apparently it's 'broken' already. What if it breaks MORE? Or gets more electricity sensitive, or becomes sensitive to something else?

(Side note, UK-class end of the world scenario hasn't been used anywhere yet so it's pretty vulnerable to being contradicted, but as they're just flufff anyway you're free to use whatever you like in any way you like.)

Also, considering that information, I don't think the Foundation would ever consider using this as a planetary defense weapon. Should probably cut that detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have been torn on whether it should be safe, euclid, or Keter, and have gotten differing advice here in the comments and in the forum help page.

They are untranslatable for the same reason the Voynich manuscript (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voynich_manuscript) is untranslatable….its just that hard where the best linguists in the world armed with computers can't crack it in years. I put the reference to the Voynich manuscript in both because I liked tieing it into a real world mystery, as well as to suggest this.

The researcher kept having stress breakdowns because of his inability to solve it even after having the alphabet and a translated passage to work off of. Knowing he should be able to crack it and then not being able to caused him stress and made him skip nghts of sleep. Imagine your job is mathamatician and you studied it for years, but someone brings in a "simple" algebra/calculous/whatever formula and you find yourself unable to solve it with super computers after days and weeks….the stress and self doubt would certainly build.

As for the size of the effect…..I put clues in concerning how big an effect it would have from a normal lightening strike…lets just say it would be a bad idea to let this happen.

I figured the researcher requests Keter since the outcome of widespread activation would be so bad. I figure the site leader who defende an attack requested it since active attacks from outside forces makes containment harder and the idea that if the Church wants it, it must be bad…these are not good enough reasons in my mind, so it was denied, but I can see someone asking for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did the researchers keep requesting it be upgraded to Keter? That seems kinda silly.

It's probably Safe, except that it's not super clear that the Foundation knows exactly how it works, meaning Euclid fits just fine. But I'm not sure the article INTENDED this. Why did the Foundation researchers have breakdowns involving this? It doesn't seem totally clear. Is there suspicion that the breakdowns were influenced by the SCP itself? Why can't they figure out how to translate it? Are they just not very good at their jobs, or it another anomalous effect of the SCP? These 'ambiguity factors' would solidly put it at Euclid.

Am I missing some reason it might be Keter? I don't quite get this opinion. I can see why people might see it as Safe, but Keter… Why?

Edit: Unless the big expunged effects are REALLY extreme and really easy to accidentally set off. That could be Keter. But it's not totally clear in the article.

Anyway, this one is just okay. No vote from me, for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed, it shouldn't be Keter, it should in fact be safe.
I wish people would read the guidelines more, it isn't how fcking dangerous it could potentially be, it's whether you can leave it in a damn box and forget about it. As long as it's shielded, this thing is safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's an SCP from the Tower of Babel biblical story? I always thought god's actions in that story were the ultimate dick moves, so my militant atheist side has just pushed me into upvoting…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disagree, both about keter and O5s.
It's not keter because you can put it in a box and walk away. Yes, enough electricity and we have "Babel 2: The Revenge"; but if you keep it away form electricity nothing happens. It's not mobile, sentient, or actively trying to escape so I don't see how it could fit keter.

O5s are humans. Over-stressed, over-worked, and probably chronically sleep-deprived humans. One of the few constants that cross every culture and era is that those on the top of the hierarchy take out their frustrations by brow-beating making the job more difficult for those below them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just say "Ate", or something. Because when you can say that "He REDACTED them over the course of a month" is anything from "He raped their eyesockets over the course of a month" to "He taught them all how to play patty-cake over the course of a month" to "He ordained them as official Rabbis over the course of a month" it's just silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are so many SCPs where an O5 senselessly berates his/her lessers, and refuses sound advice, that it's a horrible sterotype at this point. This is a good concept, but quite frankly there is no reason that this SHOULDN'T be Keter, and the real "sideshow" of the piece is the O5 communication itself.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
he is performign a one man rendition fo the Rocky Horror Picture Show, of course.

Honestly, I agree that this is not a 110-Montauk type redaction where I think people will fill in their own fear. I agree that redacting doesn't add tot he horror. But the things I would have listed there I prefered redacting IC because details of the depravity of the human condition do not interest the Foundation and OOC because including even sparse descriptions of squick makes me uncomfortable.

If you have a suggestion on a way to change it, let me know. Going too tame is weak, going too gorey is unessisary and would probably look like trying too hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I trimed it a little. I cut the reference to radiation, instead just describing its radius of effect. I prefer naming something that causes the effect, but a few people disliked it and I didn't care. I also cut the experiment log down by two, and then cut clauses and sentences whereever I could. I also removed a couple of black boxed spots for visual appeal, but I'm not sure whether I can take any more out. I could always give the researchers' names, but I'd prefer nto to.

I like the alternating current bit, but its been critisized as unneccisary. I am leaving it in for now, but will remove it if I get more negative feedback on it.

Continued feedback is helpful, especially with examples.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will change the language to not use the word Obelisk, to prevent confusion, as I have found many definitions which require obelisks to taper, as well as many obelisks that do not taper (see Obelisk of Sao Paulo). But, even if its right either way, I agree better to not confuse readers. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That picture is not an obelisk.

And that's all I have to say about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tone here is fail, specifically the references to producing wattage (do you really mean not bringing in generators and dynamos) and the whole electricity direct current bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mandatory leave was one of my favorite parts. Good overall, in my opinion, so you got my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting idea, but it needs a lot of work. Some of the wording needs to be fixed, there are too many blackboxes (I know it makes sense because you're repeating the same details/names, but it looks weird), and the bit about the microwave radiation is unnecessary. I'll upvote if/when the article is fixed.

Also, I don't understand why

Thereafter, D-20375 [REDACTED] the remaining subjects over the course of a month.

needs to be [REDACTED]. I mean, it's obvious that he played hopscotch with them. But for reals, it doesn't add to the horror to redact this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept of it, and the CotBG reference was a nice tie in, we need a little more of them. However the Class AA amnesiacs seemed… Odd. And some stuff that should've probably been blackboxed, rather than [REDACTED]. It keeps the flow going, at least for me. I'd give you the quote, but my phone doesn't like doing those.

Still was a decent read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. Almost. Then it went dumb. And I didn't like it then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "chrurch"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a lot of problems with this. Foremost, considering how straightforward the effect is, the description and specific details are convoluted as hell. The radiation is a great example- it's completely superflous to what the object actually does.
Second, certain elements the article are painfullly over the top. Bits like the researchers suffering from breakdowns after trying to study just a single language instance, or the request for all excess D-class, are completely absurd under my head-cannon, and I doubt r'm alone in that regard.
Lastly, the tone could use a lot of work. Get my attention in the IRC if you'd like specifics; I'm not prepared to post the full rundown from my phone.

Suffice to say, downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is weird. No sockets are shown in the image, for one. Class AA amnesiacs seem to be overreaching, since I've never heard of such a thing before. There are other SCPs that overwrite language ability, though that's not necessarily a downside. I dunno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but I find that the experiment log bugged me and by the end of it, most of the enthusiasm i had for the idea was gone.

I just feel like there isn't much gained from it. Still, good ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed sheepishly…..

If I post a second one, I will proof for a week instead of for a day, I promise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Request upgrade to Keter status. Dr. A████

Request denied, and pull the plug on this sideshow. If you want a long term test subject, use Dr. A████ O5-█"

Wow! He can talk english on and off! Might wanna fix that. Good story overall +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the suggestions. I am amending the radiation to the brain stem thing to sound more clinical, but I prefer it to be on contact than on immersion. Also, I like the simmilar reactions of the doctor to failing each time…the second one being a mandatory leave after a longer time studying is perhaps too small a change to be noticable, but the report would use simmilar language for simmilar results.

Does anyone else have a strong feeling either way? I will bow on either of these to a group concensus, though I am leaving them for now.

EDIT: Other things I specifically want feedback on are whether it is too long (I worry that it is, especially the containment), whether I used O5 comments correctly (I think I did, but I don't want to get their tone wrong), and whetehr this should be instead rated safe or keter (I think it is rated correctly, with denied requests for keter status).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"the moment the radiation touches the base of his brain stem"

You might change the wording to make it less overly specific. "the moment the subject's head is fully enveloped in the radiation field" or some such thing.

Also, the repetition of "Two months after testing, Dr. A████ placed on voluntary administrative leave due to stress. Subject D-18394 terminated and all documentation locked in long term storage." needs some work. I'd suggest clearly acknowledging that this has just happened again in each new report, rather than just repeating it near-verbatim.

item.title: Re: HEY FRED, LEARN TO READ
item.content:encoded: That makes a lot more sense.

item.title: Re: HEY FRED, LEARN TO READ
item.content:encoded:
You're right that additional explaination was called for, but I had read the portion you quoted, and this was on purpose.

This is not currently listed as Keter, but the costs of having it go off with Keter subjects at the same location is high enough to justify isolation. I think I clarified, including a nod to the fact that Areas are generally only for Keter class objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I had sandboxed it and let it sit in the advice forum for a while, but as advice dried up there, I figured it was good to post. I appreciate the corrections.

EDIT: Ok, thanks. I had worried I was overredacting. I will fill some of it back in. As for the CotBG, I want there to be a lot more…as it is, there is little direction, and I am open to changes, since this could very well contibute to their identity (if it sticks around). Some people seem to think the Chruch can only use steampunk type things, some have items which are metal and do stuff, but have no traditional "moving parts." Origonally, this was stone…I changed it to metal to make the Church a better fit. I made a slight change to the description to imply that the interior of the object is not a solid mass. I intended the moving parts to be just dormant, and only move when its plugged in correctly, but that's sort of a sub-plot to the story in my head about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a mess. It needs extensive tidying. But I upvoted because it's got some good ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just went through & did a lot of spelling/format cleanup, so those extraneous bars and the XXXX are fixed now.

But yeah, there's some copy-paste in the experiment logs. The wording is weird in several sections, as well.

It's an interesting take on the "Tower of Babel", but I want to see this get fixed up some more before I feel comfortable upvoting.

Oh, also? You're way overusing the black boxes & redaction. Figure out what really needs to be hidden and expose the rest.

item.title: HEY FRED, LEARN TO READ
item.content:encoded:
"██. Following Incident 1139-1, object has been moved to Research Area ██. No Keter class testing or containment is to be perfromed at Reasearch Area ██."

LET'S TAKE A LOOK AT THE FRONTSPACE.

Area: Facility built to contain Keter-class objects. Constructed far from civilian establishments, Areas house nuclear devices that can be used to clear the facility and terminate all facility personnel. These devices are detonated if the containment of a Keter-class object is breached and is not able to be contained again.

OH SHIT, HOW ABOUT THAT.

LEARN TO READ.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's potential here, but you still have some work to be done. I noticed at least one or two SCP-XXXX remaining in here, it looks like when you were copy-pasting the test logs you copied some extraneous lines between several of them, and I'm not sure what the purpose of all the extra lines between things at the bottom of the article is. The inclusion of the CoBG is nice, not enough of them around, but it almost seems a bit… Forced? That is, this thing has no real mechanical components to it aside from the fact that it uses electricity to rewrite peoples' speech center.

Still, a pretty good concept that I don't think has been done to death; fix up the typographical issues, and I'll upvote it.

For future reference, you should NEVER post into the main list until it's ready to post; if you're fixing it up, experimenting with format, etc. you should really use the sandbox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, my apologies. Only goes to show rushing is never good. I'll have a read now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's because it was incomplete. lol.
You found it literally midpost (you accidentaly locked me out for a couple minutes when you fixed the picture that I was currently working on…no harm done and I appreciate the help, though I got very confused for a second).

It is now posted, the correct picture is up, it is on the main list, and it is announced. Please let me know if something about it still feels incomplete or if you have any other advice/critisism. Its my first SCP, and after a couple of days growing in the advice thread, I decided to post it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems somewhat incomplete (like the edit I made). Have you announced this in the proper thread?
